overview.market_size: $24.4 billion globally (2024), projected $49.7B by 2034; US generates $15B+ by 2030
overview.growth_rate: 6.5% CAGR globally; 8.8% CAGR domestically (down from 12.7% in Q4 2023)
overview.fragmentation: Highly fragmented with thousands of independent operators. Consolidation accelerating via PE platforms.
overview.typical_ebitda_margin: 18%–25% for well-run operators — among the highest in home services
overview.recurring_revenue_note: Industry uniquely suited for recurring revenue models — monthly/quarterly treatment plans. Recurring revenue is the primary valuation driver.
overview.pe_interest: Extremely active — M&A split nearly 50/50 between strategic and financial buyers in 2024. Add-on transactions up 29.4% YoY.
overview.labor_challenge: Moderate — lower skill requirements than HVAC, but retention is a challenge
overview.ai_disruption_risk: Very Low — physical pest treatment resistant to automation

$2M-$5M.source: First Page Sage 2025 / Peak Business Valuation
$2M-$5M.notes: Typical range 3.26x–4.07x for smaller operators. Companies with $2-3M+ revenue and recurring contracts see 4x–6x.

$5M-$10M.notes: Revenue multiples of 3x are common shorthand, equating to ~15x free cash flow for a company with 20% margins. Quality assets in this range see robust competition.

$10M-$15M.notes: Platform-scale pest control businesses with strong recurring revenue base. Valuation premium over other service sectors.

$15M-$25M.notes: Limited public data. Pest control commands premium multiples vs. other home services due to high margins and recurring revenue.

value_drivers.explanation: Pest control is inherently recurring — monthly/quarterly treatment cycles create predictable cash flows. This is the #1 driver of premium valuations.
Low customer churn + high lifetime value. Pest control has inherently lower churn than many service businesses.
Dense service routes improve technician utilization and margins. Technology-enabled routing is a plus.
CRM, route optimization, automated scheduling, and digital payments signal operational maturity and reduce integration costs.
Termite, mosquito, wildlife, and lawn care cross-sells increase revenue per customer and stickiness.
Buyers seek management teams that can operate independently. Owner-dependent shops face significant discounts.
Residential pest control companies currently have higher multiples, though the gap is expected to narrow as commercial activity increases.

recent_transactions.rationale: Most active pest control acquirer in 2024 — aggressive roll-up strategy.
Second most active acquirer. Building national pest control platform.
Top private strategic buyer — organic and M&A driven growth.
Global leader post-Terminix acquisition. Continues tuck-in strategy.
Parent of Orkin — consistently active strategic acquirer.

pe_platforms.name: Various PE sponsors
pe_platforms.thesis: Most active PE-backed pest control platform — 7 deals in 2024 alone

pe_platforms.name: Various PE sponsors
pe_platforms.thesis: Multi-service platform including pest, lawn, and tree care — 3 deals in 2024

strategic.name: Rollins Inc. (NYSE: ROL) — parent of Orkin
strategic.strategy: Consistently active strategic acquirer. Market cap ~$20B. Acquires across all pest control segments.

strategic.name: Rentokil Initial (LSE: RTO) — owns Terminix
strategic.strategy: Global #1 after acquiring Terminix for $6.7B in 2022. Continues tuck-in acquisitions.

strategic.name: Arrow Exterminators
strategic.strategy: Largest privately-held US pest control company — 6 acquisitions in 2024. Family-owned, aggressive acquirer.

strategic.name: Massey Services
strategic.strategy: Florida-based private strategic — 3 acquisitions in 2024. Pest control, lawn, and termite.
